content:
- `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed.
- `make test` initially failed because the Rust verify-source contract still assumed the vendored Go slice had only `verify`; I fixed that by explicitly allowing the new `verifyservice` package and by moving the command tests under `cmd/verifyservice` instead of leaving a stray top-level cmd test file.
- Updated task 04 and its execution plan to completed/checked, then ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully.
- Next: review final git status, commit all task files and code changes with the required task-finished message, push, and stop.

- Finished story 18 task 04 end-to-end.
- Implemented the dedicated verify-service config boundary, validated it with red/green Go tests, updated the Rust verify-source contract for the new allowed verifyservice surface, and removed the old dbconn.Config carrier.
- Required lanes are green: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.
- Updated the task + plan files, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed as `e1d2e22`, and pushed to `master`.
- Workspace is clean; stop here.

- Read the choose-task rules, refreshed the Ralph summaries, and surveyed the repo layout.
- The actual codebase is a Rust workspace with runner/source-bootstrap crates plus a vendored Go cockroachdb_molt verify surface; active work is on image/runtime split stories.
- Next: inspect active task files for blocked_by/priority metadata, resolve against current_tasks_done, then write the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Refreshed Ralph summaries, checked the choose-task rules, inspected active task metadata for blockers/priorities, and surveyed the real repo architecture.
- There are no active failing bug tasks; the only explicit active blocker is story-22a/02 blocked by story-22a/01. The repo still exposes novice-user repo-clone/local-build paths in README and the active image/runtime split stories, so the most logical next unblocked failing task is story-16 task 04.
- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt with .ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/04-task-remove-novice-user-dependence-on-repo-clone-and-local-tooling.md. Next agent should execute that task with the tdd skill, then run make check, make test, and make lint.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the local TDD/improve-code-boundaries skill docs.
- Current task is story 16 task 04: remove novice-user dependence on repo clone and local tooling.
- Next I am reading the task file and nearby plan artifacts to determine whether I must create/adjust a plan or execute an existing NOW EXECUTE plan.

- Read the task file plus prior story plan style and started tracing the novice-user contract surface.
- Confirmed README still tells users to run `cargo run -p source-bootstrap` and `docker build -t cockroach-migrate-runner .`, while CI currently publishes only one runner image.
- Next I am checking neighboring story/task docs to decide whether task 04 should introduce a published source-bootstrap image boundary or remove that source-bootstrap path from the novice quick start entirely before I write the plan.

- Gathered enough context to plan task 04.
- Key design decision: the novice-user path should move to published-image coordinates, likely by adding a dedicated source-bootstrap image boundary and by splitting README/public image assertions away from local docker build harness helpers.
- Next I am creating the task 04 plan file, adding its path to the task record, and marking the plan `NOW EXECUTE` because the interface direction is concrete enough for the next turn to implement via red/green slices.

- Created task 04 execution plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/04-task-remove-novice-user-dependence-on-repo-clone-and-local-tooling_plans/2026-04-19-published-images-only-novice-path-plan.md`.
- Added the `<plan>` path to the task file and marked the plan `NOW EXECUTE` because the interface direction is clear: published images only, dedicated source-bootstrap image boundary, dual-image CI publish, and cleanup of the mixed README-vs-local-harness docker contract.
- Stopping now per task instructions; next turn should execute the plan via red/green slices, then run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, with `make test-long` only if ignored long tests or their selection are touched.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill docs.
- Confirmed the current task is story 16 task 04 and that the prior turn already created a `NOW EXECUTE` plan for it.
- Next I am reading the task file and linked execution plan, then I will implement the work in red/green slices and keep appending progress during execution.

- Read the task file and linked `NOW EXECUTE` plan, then inspected the current README, CI workflow, runner README/public-contract helpers, and source-bootstrap tests/code.
- Added the first red TDD slice for a dedicated source-bootstrap image boundary: new `crates/source-bootstrap/tests/image_contract.rs` plus source-bootstrap-owned image contract and harness support files.
- Ran `cargo test -p source-bootstrap --test image_contract`; it failed exactly where expected because `crates/source-bootstrap/Dockerfile` does not exist yet, confirming the missing public image boundary.
- Next I am adding the source-bootstrap Dockerfile and making the new image test pass before moving on to the README and workflow slices.

- Implemented and greened the dedicated `source-bootstrap` image boundary with `crates/source-bootstrap/Dockerfile` and a new containerized image contract test.
- Split README/public image assertions away from `runner_docker_contract.rs` into README-owned support, rewrote the README novice quick starts around `GITHUB_OWNER` + `IMAGE_TAG` + `docker pull` + published image references, and removed the local `cargo run` / `docker build` operator path.
- Extended `.github/workflows/master-image.yml` and its CI contract to publish both novice-path images (`runner` and `source-bootstrap`) with separate build archives, Trivy scans, reports, loads, and pushes under the same trusted master-only gate.
- Next I am removing any remaining novice-path test leakage that still treats the README source-bootstrap flow as a local CLI invocation, then I will run the required repository lanes (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`) and finish task bookkeeping if they pass.
